content.description:
Hobson uses JSON-based import/export for data portability, backup, and development workflows. The import system is smart — it handles dependencies, avoids breaking running code, and applies the minimal set of changes.

## Export

### Single Item Export

```javascript
await api.export(itemId);  // Downloads one item as JSON
```

### Multi-Item Export

```javascript
await api.export();  // Downloads all items as JSON array
```

Exported files include all item fields. The format is the same JSON structure used in `src/items/*.json`.

## Import

### Smart Import with Topological Sort

When importing items that reference each other (e.g., a view references its type), the import system sorts items in dependency order:

1. Parse the JSON (single item or array)
2. Build a dependency graph (type references, `extends` references)
3. Topologically sort — types before instances, parent types before subtypes
4. Write items in dependency order so validation passes

This means you can export a set of related items and import them into a fresh system without worrying about ordering.

### Conflict Handling

When an imported item has the same ID as an existing item:
- The import overwrites the existing item
- This is the standard behavior for updating items from file

## Hot-Reload Logic

After import, the system determines the minimal reload needed:

| What Changed | Reload Action |
|-------------|---------------|
| Kernel module | Full page reload |
| kernel:styles | Replace `<style>` element only |
| View code | Re-render affected instances |
| Library | Clear module cache entry |
| Data item | Re-render if currently visible |

This means importing an updated view doesn't require a full page reload — only the visible instances of that view re-render.

## Development Workflow

The standard development workflow with Claude Code:

1. Edit JSON files in `src/items/*.json`
2. Import updated files into running Hobson
3. Changes take effect immediately (with appropriate hot-reload)
4. Export from Hobson to capture any in-system changes
5. Commit JSON files to git

## File Format

Single item:
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "name": "Item Name",
  "type": "type-uuid",
  "created": 1706000000000,
  "modified": 1706000000000,
  "content": { ... },
  "attachments": []
}
```

Multiple items:
```json
[
  { "id": "...", ... },
  { "id": "...", ... }
]
```

## See Also

- [Bootstrap](item://c0c0c0c0-0002-0000-0000-000000000000) — First-run import flow
- [Safe Mode](item://c0c0c0c0-0007-0000-0000-000000000000) — Export/import in recovery
- [REPL](item://c0c0c0c0-0008-0000-0000-000000000000) — `api.import()` and `api.export()` commands
